items.text: Fire alarm testing will be conducted in Maple Hall on Tuesday, May 12, from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM. Residents are advised to remain indoors. Replacement detectors are available from the front desk for a $5 deposit, refunded upon return.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should residents do during the testing period?
questions.explanation: The notice states residents are advised to remain indoors during the 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM testing.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $5 deposit?

options.A: It covers the cost of the alarm test
options.B: It is charged to every resident automatically
options.C: It must be paid before May 12
options.D: It will be returned when the detector is given back

questions.explanation: The notice says the deposit is refunded upon return, so residents get the money back once they return the detector.

items.text: The recycling bins outside Carter Library will be relocated to the east entrance of Student Union beginning Monday, June 1. The current location closes Friday, May 29, for repaving. Residents are asked to direct any questions to the Facilities Office at extension 4400 before the scheduled move.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where will the recycling bins be relocated?

options.C: The east entrance of Student Union

questions.explanation: The notice states the bins will be moved to the east entrance of Student Union starting June 1.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the current bin location?

options.A: It will be unavailable for several days
options.B: It will remain open through June
options.C: It is being expanded for more bins

questions.explanation: The old spot closes May 29 for repaving and new bins open June 1, so there is a gap when the location is unavailable.

items.text: ONE NIGHT ONLY! The Drama Club proudly presents "Midnight Echo," a thrilling student-written mystery. Friday, April 18, at 7:30 PM in Bowen Theater. Tickets are just $5 at the door. Limited seating—arrive early to claim your spot! All proceeds support next season's student productions.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, how much does a ticket cost?
questions.explanation: The flyer states tickets are just $5 at the door.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about attending the show?

options.A: Coming early improves the chance of a seat
options.B: Tickets must be reserved online beforehand
options.C: Additional performances will be added later

questions.explanation: Seating is limited and the flyer urges attendees to arrive early, implying early arrival helps secure a seat.

items.text: Unclaimed items currently held in the Lost and Found at Hill Center will be donated to a local charity after Friday, March 6. Owners may retrieve their belongings from Room 110 anytime before 4:00 PM that day. A $2 handling fee applies to electronics only.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what fee is mentioned?

options.A: A storage fee for all items
options.B: A late fee after the deadline

questions.explanation: The notice states a $2 handling fee applies to electronics only.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about items left after the deadline?

options.A: They are returned to the front desk
options.B: They are stored for another month
options.C: They will no longer be available to owners
options.D: They are sold at a campus auction

questions.explanation: Items are donated to charity after March 6, so owners can no longer retrieve them past the deadline.
